channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Marvel Rivals Switch 2 Version Officially Confirmed by NetEase
item.content:encoded:
NetEase has confirmed that popular hero shooter Marvel Rivals will make its way to Switch 2 at some point in the future, although the company hasn't revealed a release date or even a rough estimate of when we can expect the port.

During the recent Game Developers Conference (GDC), IGN's Rebekah Valentine asked Marvel exec Danny Koo if Marvel Rivals would come to Switch 2, to which Koo replied that the studio is "working on it" and will "announce that one" when it has news to share.

Valentine then repeated her question with different wording to confirm that a Switch 2 version of Marvel Rivals is on the cards, to which Koo responded "we will do it". It's hard to get more unequivocal (less equivocal?) than that.

Given that Overwatch (formerly known as Overwatch 2, which was formerly known as Overwatch) will be making its way to the Switch 2 at some point in the future as well, bringing Marvel Rivals to the console makes a lot of sense.

However, since NetEase has neglected to announce a release date, it could be a while before we hear from this one, so don't expect to be loading it onto your console in the next few months, at least.

This news comes shortly after the reveal of Marvel Rivals' seventh season, which will introduce Strategist support character White Fox as its first new playable hero. The second, Black Cat, will come sometime after, and she'll presumably be a Duelist, although we don't know yet.

The two fighters follow on from other characters introduced as part of seasonal updates, with Marvel stalwarts like Gambit, Rogue, and Daredevil debuting in previous content drops.

While you wait for Marvel Rivals to arrive on Switch 2, you can check it out right now on PC, PlayStation, and Xbox Series X|S.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/marvel-rivals-switch-2-confirmed

item.title: PlayStation Shuts Down COD Veteran's Dark Outlaw Games
item.content:encoded:
Dark Outlaw Games, the studio founded by Call of Duty veteran Jason Blundell, has been shut down by parent PlayStation, becoming the latest in a long line of industry lifer-led studios to close.

The studio's closure was first revealed by Bloomberg's Jason Schreier on Bluesky before being confirmed by Sony in a statement to PC Gamer, albeit in a somewhat roundabout way; Sony refers to "several strategic adjustments to support long-term sustainability", as is the usual corporate line.

Blundell first revealed Dark Outlaw back in March last year, announcing at the same time that he and the studio were "working away in the shadows" on their first project, which had, at the time, been in the works for a while.

Unless Blundell or one of the people working at Dark Outlaw decide to release information pertaining to that debut project (which is unlikely), we'll probably never learn exactly what he and the studio were working on.

However, we can make an educated guess that the game was most likely some sort of first-person shooter; Blundell's experience over the last 14 years or so is mostly in Call of Duty, so it stands to reason Sony would want to bring that experience to bear on a new project.

Dark Outlaw is the latest studio to close under Sony's auspices, and it's the second this year, following on from the shutting down of Demon's Souls and Shadow of the Colossus remake developer Bluepoint.

They both join studios like Jake Solomon's Midsummer, long-running outfits Twisted Pixel and Sanzaru Games, and ex-Ubisoft producer Sean Crooks' Bad Brain Studios, and that's without even mentioning the glut of studios that have laid off employees recently.

We'll have to wait and see what Blundell moves onto next, but we may well not have seen the last of him. Let's hope he can salvage something from the ashes of Dark Outlaw, at any rate. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/playstation-dark-outlaw-games-shutdown
